dataset: OrientDB DemoDB with Real Hotel Data

pi_items.source: Valid OrientDB spatial index naming convention per https://orientdb.dev/docs/develop/indexing/Spatial-Index.html
Task specification defines this as the MarketingZone value to set for hotels within the polygon

Real hotel in Rome, Italy. Web search confirms Hotel Artemide location at ~41.9009°N, 12.4935°E. Seed data: 41.8981°N, 12.4989°E. Both coordinates are within the polygon bounds (12.48-12.51 longitude, 41.89-41.91 latitude). Hotel is on Via Nazionale 22, Rome.

Real hotel in Berlin, Germany. Web search confirms Hotel Adlon Kempinski at 52.516°N, 13.380°E. Seed data: 52.5163°N, 13.3791°E. Coordinates are clearly outside Rome polygon bounds (Rome is at ~41.89°N, 12.51°E).

Verified against Rome geographic coordinates. Rome center is at 41.89193°N, 12.51133°E (per web search). The polygon bounds [41.89-41.91°N, 12.48-12.51°E] correctly cover central Rome historic center.

privileged_info_summary: The spatial geofencing task uses real hotel data: Hotel Artemide is a real 4-star boutique hotel located in Rome at Via Nazionale 22 (coordinates ~41.8981°N, 12.4989°E, confirmed to be 41.9009°N, 12.4935°E via web search). Hotel Adlon Kempinski is a real 5-star luxury hotel in Berlin at Unter den Linden 77 (52.5163°N, 13.3791°E). The rectangular polygon (12.48-12.51°E, 41.89-41.91°N) correctly represents the historic center of Rome, with Hotel Artemide properly positioned inside the bounds while Hotel Adlon Kempinski is correctly positioned outside (in Berlin). The database 'demodb' is based on OrientDB's official DemoDB schema but populated with real hotel data.
